title: Prayer for Ramadan, by Lior bar-Ami
author: Lior Bar-Ami

coauthors.name: Lior Bar-Ami
coauthors.bio: Lior Bar-Ami is a Jewish writer and liturgical creator based in Europe. Ordained in 2016 at the Abraham Geiger College in Berlin, he previously served in rabbinic roles in France and Austria. He no longer works as a rabbi and does not identify as religious. His work has evolved toward a humanistic and spiritual expression of Jewish life, creating contemporary liturgy that centers shared humanity, ethical responsibility, memory, and solidarity. His texts seek to open reflective spaces across traditions — grounded in Jewish language and experience, yet not confined to religious doctrine.

excerpt: This prayer for Jewish-Muslim meeting on Ramadan was written by Lior bar-Ami and first published via his Facebook page on 18 February 2026.
content:
Contribute a translation Source (English)
May this season of Ramadan
meet us at the place
where the human heart is most awake.
As Jews, we are a people who know
the sacred rhythm of pausing —
of stepping out of the ordinary flow of life
to remember who we are
and who we hope to become.
When others turn toward
reflection,
restraint,
and generosity,
may we recognize a familiar path:
the quiet work of refining the soul.
May this month remind us
that holiness is not owned by any one tradition.
It appears wherever people choose
compassion over indifference,
humility over certainty,
and care over separation.
May we learn from the courage it takes to fast —
the courage to say:
I am more than my impulses.
I can make space within myself.
I can live with intention.
Let this be a time for an expansion of the heart.
Wide enough to hold complexity.
Steady enough to resist hatred.
Tender enough to remain moved
by the suffering of others.
May we remember
that every human being is created
in the image of shared dignity —
and that our task is not to agree on everything,
but to guard that dignity
wherever it is threatened.
Where there is suspicion,
may we bring curiosity.
Where there is pain,
may we bring presence.
Where there are walls,
may we become doorways.
Like Abraham, whose tent was open on every side,
may we cultivate a radical hospitality of spirit —
making room in our moral imagination
for neighbors we do not fully know,
yet are bound to nonetheless.
And may this month call forth
the best of who we are:
A people committed
to memory,
to conscience,
to responsibility —
and to the stubborn, enduring belief
that the world can still be repaired.

This prayer for Jewish-Muslim meeting on Ramadan was written by Lior bar-Ami and first published via his Facebook page on 18 February 2026.
